svg.title: hashCode() deep dive — what it returns and how bit spreading works
svg.desc: Two focused diagrams: what hashCode() returns with examples, and a step-by-step bit spreading walkthrough showing why Java applies hash XOR hash-right-shift-16.
svg.text: Returns a 32-bit signed integer — range: -2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647
HashMap uses this integer to decide which bucket the key goes into
Why bit spreading? The problem first

g.text: Problem: hashCode() high bits are ignored
index = hash & (n-1) only uses LOW bits → poor hashes cluster in same buckets

svg.text: Two keys with different high bits but same low bits → same bucket (collision!)
How bit spreading works — step by step

g.text: Moves high 16 bits → low 16 bits position
Mixes high bits into low bits → more unique low bits
